title: Studio Historical Timeline
organization: Two Marshalls Studios — Studio Operating System (TMS-OS)
summary: Preserves the verified development sequence of Two Marshalls Studios and TMS-OS.

sections.title: Purpose
paragraphs: This document preserves the verified historical development sequence of Two Marshalls Studios and the Studio Operating System (TMS-OS). It provides a permanent human-readable timeline of major foundation stages, completed system milestones, the current recovery period, and the transition into permanent Google documentation.
This timeline is based on recovered and verified records. Where an exact calendar date is not verified, the sequence is preserved without inventing a date.

sections.title: Historical Control Rule
paragraphs: Only verified historical events may be recorded as established history.

Unknown dates, unsupported milestones, and assumed development events must not be added. Future ideas belong in ENH-LOG-001 and do not become historical events until they are actually approved, completed, and verified.

sections.title: Origin and Foundation Period
paragraphs: The studio began establishing a centralized operating system so games, systems, publishing, documentation, and studio operations could be managed through a shared command structure.
items: Two Marshalls Studios established TMS-OS as the central operating framework.
The Studio Portal became the main visible command center for studio information and operations.
A structured studio data layer was developed to prevent important information from remaining scattered across individual pages and files.
The long-term direction required studio work to be accessible across multiple workstations rather than depending on one computer.

sections.title: Studio Database Foundation
paragraphs: This period established the core structured data foundation that later documentation, headquarters, search, and operating-system functions could use.

sections.title: Documentation and Headquarters Integration
paragraphs: These stages moved the studio toward a single governed information structure rather than separate page-specific sources.

sections.title: Data Consolidation and Cleanup
paragraphs: This work reduced fragmentation and prepared the studio data for broader search and cross-department use.

sections.title: Shared Search Development
paragraphs: The shared search engine became capable of searching across studio knowledge rather than remaining limited to one department or page.

sections.title: Fully Modular StudioDB Milestone
paragraphs: MILE-013 - Fully Modular StudioDB
MILE-013 is the last verified completed development milestone. It established the current architecture as Fully Modular StudioDB.

sections.title: Transition to Historical Recovery
paragraphs: After the modular StudioDB milestone, the studio identified a critical need to preserve and reconcile historical knowledge that still existed across chat history, temporary files, screenshots, backups, and other non-permanent sources.
items: Current work: Historical Recovery and Documentation Reconciliation
Current active milestone: MILE-014 - Complete Historical Recovery

sections.title: Google Headquarters Structure Established
paragraphs: During MILE-014, the permanent Google Headquarters structure was created and verified.
The Google Headquarters structure is verified as complete.

sections.title: Permanent Documentation Population Begins
paragraphs: The studio then began a controlled process for creating permanent human-readable Google documents from verified recovery evidence.
At this checkpoint, 8 permanent Google documents were verified before HIST-001 was created.

sections.title: Documentation Recovery Framework
paragraphs: Historical recovery identified 16 permanent documentation categories requiring coverage. These categories are coverage areas, not a fixed numbered upload sequence.

sections.title: Procedures and Governance Established During Recovery
items: PROC-001 established the permanent Work Session Procedure.
PROC-002 established the permanent Bug Fix Procedure.
PROC-003 established the permanent Documentation Update Procedure.
ENH-LOG-001 established permanent enhancement tracking from the beginning of the project.
DOC-INDEX-001 established the permanent document control index.
ROADMAP-001 established the verified roadmap and separation between active work and deferred enhancements.

sections.title: Current Historical Checkpoint
items: Last completed development milestone: MILE-013 - Fully Modular StudioDB
Current active milestone: MILE-014 - Complete Historical Recovery

sections.title: Current Timeline Position
paragraphs: The studio is currently in the historical recovery and documentation reconciliation period.

The immediate work is to continue recovering the remaining documentation categories one verified document at a time, place each permanent document in the approved Google Headquarters location, verify it, and update the documentation control records.

sections.title: Relationship to Enhancement History
paragraphs: Enhancement ideas discovered during historical recovery are preserved in ENH-LOG-001. They must be captured from the beginning of the project, but they remain deferred unless Stephen explicitly approves them for active work.
The historical timeline records what happened. The Enhancement Ideas Log preserves what may happen later.

sections.title: Historical Completion Standard
paragraphs: A historical event is considered permanently recovered when its supporting evidence has been reconciled, its meaning has been preserved in the appropriate permanent record, and the result no longer depends only on temporary chat history, screenshots, local files, or memory.

sections.title: Permanent Statement
paragraphs: The Two Marshalls Studios historical timeline must remain a verified record of the studio's actual development path. It exists so future work can understand how the studio reached its current state without inventing history, losing completed work, or confusing deferred ideas with verified accomplishments.

revisionHistory.summary: Connected the verified permanent document to the shared structured Document Template Engine.

verification.method: Converted from the recovered DOCX source used to create the verified Google Headquarters document.
